behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in local parsing/segmentation logic itself, not a module handoff requirement. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; input is a command string and output is segment list. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration/System because behavior text is only about segmentation of newline-separated statements, with no persistence, subprocess, or external boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Call the segmenter on a multiline command block and assert it returns distinct ordered segments (assignments and `gh ...` command separated), rather than one monolithic segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior targets trigger-classification logic over parsed segments. It can fail entirely within parser+matcher logic without I/O. REAL-INFRA: no real shell execution is required by behavior text; only recognition. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration/System because the behavior only requires recognition of `gh pr create` in a multiline block, not file/state handoff or subprocess effects.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a multiline command fixture with variable-assignment lines then `gh pr create ...`; assert classifier result is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: negative classification boundary is local matching logic (`pr create` vs `issue create`). REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess needed; pure input/output classification. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration/System because behavior text is command-type discrimination only, with no cross-module persistence or real infrastructure dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a multiline block ending in `gh issue create ...` and assert classifier does not emit PR-create trigger.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is module handoff (trigger payload -> PR id reconstruction -> state persistence) plus local filesystem write. Unit-only tests of one function can miss serialization/path contract issues. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) real OS/network/subprocess not required beyond local file write/read, so not forced to System; (b) no AI output dependency; (c) no multi-agent chain; (d) no LLM-governed orchestration. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text says 'persist ... state' and does not require real subprocess/HTTP/OS event behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the post-tool hook path in-process with a temp state directory and a PR-create trigger event; assert persisted state contains the reconstructed PR identifier in the expected field/path.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is explicitly a cross-module contract (PostToolUse writer -> PreToolUse reader/enforcer). Real failure appears at handoff boundary; unit tests of either side alone can pass while contract breaks. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) does not require real network/subprocess/OS primitives beyond local file contract, so Integration is sufficient; (b) no AI/ML dependency; (c) no multi-agent sequence; (d) not LLM-orchestrated. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text is about readable/enforceable state format between hooks, not real external infrastructure conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute post-tool detection and state write, then invoke pre-tool enforcement using the same temp state location; assert pre-tool hook reads the written state and applies enforcement decision tied to that PR context.
